samples.xml_description:
Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. Clock. Reset. 1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0). 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division. 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division. 1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present. 1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged. 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready. 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits. 0 1 2

Initialize the divider module. If the operation is signed, extend the sign of the dividend and divisor. Otherwise, use them as is.

Enter the DIVIDE state when a valid operation request is detected.

Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. If the operation is signed, handle the sign.

Enter the DONE state when the division is complete.

Reset to the IDLE state when the reset signal is asserted.

 Initialize the divider module. Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. Enter the DONE state when the division is complete.

Initialize the divider module. If the operation is signed, extend the sign of the dividend and divisor. Otherwise, use them as is.

Enter the DIVIDE state when a valid operation request is detected.

Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. If the operation is signed, handle the sign.

Enter the DONE state when the division is complete.

Reset to the IDLE state when the reset signal is asserted.

 Initialize the divider module. if (rst) begin state <= S_IDLE; count <= 0; result <= 0; remainder <= 0; end Enter the DIVIDE state when a valid operation request is detected. else if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin state <= S_DIVIDE; count <= 0; if (sign & dividend[7]) begin remainder <= -dividend; end else begin remainder <= dividend; end if (sign & divisor[7]) begin divisor_reg <= -divisor; end else begin divisor_reg <= divisor; end end Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. else if (state == S_DIVIDE) begin if (count == 8) begin state <= S_DONE; end else begin count <= count + 1; if (remainder >= 0) begin remainder <= (remainder << 1) | (SR[15] & 1); SR <= (SR << 1) | (remainder >= 0 ? 1 : 0); remainder <= remainder - divisor_reg; end else begin remainder <= (remainder << 1) | (SR[15] & 1); SR <= (SR << 1) | (remainder >= 0 ? 1 : 0); remainder <= remainder + divisor_reg; end end end Enter the DONE state when the division is complete. else if (state == S_DONE) begin state <= S_IDLE; count <= 0; result <= {remainder, SR[7:0]}; end
